evals.name: product-reviews-embed-vs-reference
evals.prompt: I'm building an e-commerce platform and I'm stuck on how to model product reviews. Each product can have anywhere from 0 to maybe 5000 reviews (most products have under 100 though). Each review has: reviewer name, rating (1-5 stars), review text (up to 2000 chars), helpful votes count, and timestamp. On the product page, I show the product details plus the 10 most recent reviews, and there's a "see all reviews" link that takes you to a paginated list. I also have a separate "My Reviews" page where users can see all their own reviews across all products. Should I embed the reviews in the product document or keep them in a separate reviews collection? I'm worried about the 16MB limit but also don't want to do unnecessary lookups.
evals.expected_output: A clear recommendation on whether to embed or reference, with specific reasoning about: 1) Access patterns (reviews shown with product vs independently), 2) Cardinality (unbounded growth potential with popular products), 3) Document size concerns (5000 reviews would exceed 16MB), 4) Query patterns (pagination, user's reviews across products). Should recommend a hybrid or reference approach given the unbounded nature and independent access patterns.

evals.name: reviews-minimal-context
evals.prompt: I'm building an e-commerce platform with mongodb, currently planning a product reviews feature. I'm not sure whether to embed reviews in the product document or keep them in a separate collection.
evals.expected_output: Should ask clarifying questions about key MongoDB schema design factors before giving a recommendation. Important questions: 1) Cardinality (typical and max reviews per product), 2) Boundedness (can grow indefinitely or has hard limit - the unbounded array anti-pattern), 3) Access patterns (always with product vs independently), 4) Document size concerns (16MB limit). Should NOT give a firm recommendation without gathering this information.

assertions.name: asks_about_cardinality
assertions.description: Asks about typical and maximum number of reviews per product (e.g., 'most have 10, some have 1000')

assertions.name: asks_about_boundedness
assertions.description: Asks whether review growth is bounded (hard limit) or unbounded (can grow indefinitely) - the critical unbounded array anti-pattern

assertions.name: asks_about_access_patterns
assertions.description: Asks how reviews are accessed: always with product (embed candidate) vs independently/separately (reference candidate)

assertions.name: mentions_document_size_concerns
assertions.description: Mentions 16MB limit, document size concerns, or provides size calculations

assertions.name: avoids_premature_recommendation
assertions.description: Should not give a definitive recommendation without gathering these key requirements first

evals.name: blog-comments-outlier-pattern
evals.prompt: I'm modeling blog posts and comments. Most posts have 5-20 comments, but a few viral posts have 50,000+ comments. Should I embed the comments in the post document or use a separate collection?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as an outlier scenario and suggest the Outlier Pattern: embed comments for normal posts (5-20 comments) for performance, but use a separate collection or overflow strategy for viral posts that exceed a threshold. Pure separation works but misses optimization opportunity for the common case (95%+ of posts).
